question_text: Which of the following is an example of a non-contact force?

options.label: A
options.text: Friction between a shoe and the ground

options.label: B
options.text: The pull of Earth's gravity on a falling object

options.label: C
options.text: The normal force from a chair pushing upwards on you

options.label: D
options.text: Air resistance acting on a parachute

explanation: Gravity is a non-contact force because it acts between objects even when they are not touching. The other options (friction, normal force, and air resistance) are all contact forces that require direct physical contact between surfaces.
